relative_path: Researchers Visit/Prof. Alan D_Andrea_s visit to Helsinki/Buying 4-day-ticket in HSL app.docx
full_text:
For your stay, we recommend a 4 day ticket as it is the most convenient. 4-day-ticket is 20€ per adult and paid in the app from the debit/card.

The app is available in English. If app downloads in Finnish, you can easily change settings from “Lisää” next to three lines-icon, “Asetukset”->”Kieli”->English

How to buy 4-day-ticket:

Tickets->Day tickets. Then select as in the picture on the left.

<-Here, selected are AB travel zone (which covers all Helsinki), adult as customer type, that ticket starts immediately and that its duration is 4 days.

If buying a ticket on Friday evening: remember to select validity starts only on Saturday morning to be able to use the ticket for Tuesday as well!

For payment method you have to select “Add payment card” to be able to add your card details.

Notice: app moves you to separate (Finnish) site to fill in details, however it is safe to use it as this part of public service (substantial part of public transport is tax-funded in Finland)

The page for adding card details is in Finnish, add card number (kortin numero), date and year of expiration (kortti voimassa, kk and vv) and safecode CVC.

When details are correct, press “Jatka” (continue) to get back to the app. Now you can buy the 4-day-ticket =) In the buying process one needs to be patient and whenever one sees any loading-circles, do not close the app.

When your ticket is valid, the ticket-page shows a moving icon with changing colour background + QR-code on Tickets-page and ticket details-

Notice: Every traveler needs an own ticket, perhaps the handiest way is to download their own app for everyone. Tickets need to be shown to drivers in buses (except orange ones) and to be kept available (be sure to load your smartphone, app consumes relatively much battery) whenever using transports as ticket inspection is possible.

TIPS: When discovering the town on your own you can search public transportation options from “Routes” by adding start and destination + desired time. After that, a list of options appears, and from Settings of that page more criteria can be filled in.
